articles.title: How S&P Global’s (SPGI) Mobility Spin Off and Debt Raise Could Reshape Its Core Story
articles.summary: S&P Global is spinning off its Mobility division into Mobility Global Inc., backed by a $2.00 billion notes offering and a $500 million credit facility. This separation aims to sharpen S&P Global's focus on its core financial infrastructure businesses. The move is expected to clarify its investment narrative around data, benchmarks, and ratings, though its reliance on active equity and debt markets will continue to influence revenue.

articles.title: Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) Is Up 5.0% After Beat, Cost Cuts, Activist Deal And Insider Buys
articles.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH) saw its stock rise 5.0% after reporting first-quarter results that exceeded guidance and announcing $125 million in SG&A run-rate savings. The company also reached a cooperation agreement with activist investor Elliott Management, leading to board changes and enhanced oversight, while two directors made significant insider stock purchases. Despite the positive news, a law firm is investigating potential securities-related claims due to reduced full-year adjusted EPS guidance.

articles.title: Verizon at $48: A Debt-Laden Value Trap or the Ultimate Cash Machine?
articles.summary: Verizon has seen a 12.1% price return over the past year and, despite market concerns about its debt, is undergoing a structural transformation due to reduced capital expenditures on 5G infrastructure. This shift is freeing up significant free cash flow, allowing the company to maintain a 5.9% dividend yield and aggressively de-risk its balance sheet. A TIKR valuation model at its current price of $48.35 suggests a strong defensive entry point for long-term investors, projecting an 8.7% annualized internal rate of return based on conservative growth assumptions.

articles.title: How Investors Are Reacting To Valley National Bancorp (VLY) Refinancing Debt While Maintaining Its Dividend
articles.summary: Valley National Bancorp recently completed a $500 million fixed-to-floating subordinated notes offering and redeemed $300 million in existing notes, while affirming its quarterly common dividend of $0.11 per share. This financial maneuvering aims to restructure its funding mix and maintain shareholder distributions. The article notes that while these actions tidy up Tier 2 capital and maintain the dividend, investors should be aware of the bank's concentration in commercial real estate, which poses credit quality risks amidst broader regional bank pressures.

articles.summary: VinFast Auto is planning to sell two factories in Vietnam to reduce approximately US$6.9 billion in debt, while also launching upgraded electric SUVs and expanding global aftermarket support. Despite a recent 18.12% drop in its 30-day share price, the stock is trading below the US$6.30 analyst price target, driven by efforts to expand charging infrastructure, improve cost efficiency, and scale revenue. However, the company faces risks such as heavy cash burn, reliance on Vingroup, limited brand recognition outside Vietnam, and intense global EV competition.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers targeted US, Israel and UAE, Palo Alto Networks says
articles.summary: An Iran-linked cyber espionage group, identified as Screening Serpens (also known as UNC1549, Smoke Sandstorm, and Iranian Dream Job), launched a months-long campaign targeting entities in the US, Israel, and the UAE. Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 reported the group used six new remote access Trojan variants and a novel attack technique called AppDomainManager hijacking. The attacks, which saw two coordinated waves of cyberattacks between February and April 2026, often leveraged social engineering with fake recruitment lures to trick technology professionals.
articles.content: An Iran-linked cyber espionage group, identified as Screening Serpens (also known as UNC1549, Smoke Sandstorm, and Iranian Dream Job), launched a months-long campaign targeting entities in the US, Israel, and the UAE. Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 reported the group used six new remote access Trojan variants and a novel attack technique called AppDomainManager hijacking. The attacks, which saw two coordinated waves of cyberattacks between February and April 2026, often leveraged social engineering with fake recruitment lures to trick technology professionals.

articles.title: Is It Smart To Buy Expedia Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:EXPE) Before It Goes Ex-Dividend?

The company's dividend appears sustainable, as it pays out only 14% of its profit after tax and 5.0% of its free cash flow. With strong earnings growth of 61% annually over the last five years and a 7.2% average annual dividend increase over the past decade, Expedia Group presents a promising profile for dividend investors despite a past dividend cut.

The company's dividend appears sustainable, as it pays out only 14% of its profit after tax and 5.0% of its free cash flow. With strong earnings growth of 61% annually over the last five years and a 7.2% average annual dividend increase over the past decade, Expedia Group presents a promising profile for dividend investors despite a past dividend cut.

articles.summary: Whirlpool Corp. recently provided investors with updated quarterly figures and insights into demand for its home appliances. The article examines the key drivers influencing Whirlpool's revenue and cost structure, including macroeconomic conditions, product mix, raw material costs, and regional market dynamics. It also discusses the company's competitive positioning within the home appliance industry and its relevance for US investors keen on housing and consumer spending trends.

articles.title: First Trust Growth Strength ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:FTGS) $FTGS
articles.summary: The First Trust Growth Strength ETF (FTGS) tracks an equal-weighted index of 50 large-cap US companies known for strong market positions and growth potential. The ETF, launched in October 2022 and managed by First Trust, has seen its stock price increase by 2.2% this year, trading at $36.13 as of May 8, 2026. Major institutional investors include Assetmark Inc. and Park National Corp OH.

articles.title: UBS Group AG stock (CH0244767585): focus on strategic expansion after recent quarterly update
articles.summary: UBS Group AG is navigating a pivotal phase as it integrates Credit Suisse, manages regulatory expectations, and reinforces its position as a global wealth manager. Recent results for the fourth quarter and full year 2024 showed both progress in cost synergies and strong wealth management inflows, along with the impact of integration expenses. The article highlights UBS's business model, revenue drivers, industry trends, and its relevance for US investors, who can access the stock through its NYSE listing.
